2606.31334.title: Optimization Algorithms for Joint OFDM Waveform Design and RIS Configuration in 6G Networks: From Convex Relaxation to Foundation Models
2606.31334.paper_summary: Joint OFDM-RIS optimization for 6G is a mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem covering sum-rate maximization, energy efficiency, max-min fairness, and peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR)-constrained objectives. Seventy-eight joint OFDM-RIS optimization works published between 2021 and 2026 are surveyed. No standardized benchmark exists, and cross-paper comparisons remain infeasible. This survey classifies these works into four paradigms: (I) model-based convex relaxation, (II) heuristic and metaheuristic search, (III) deep reinforcement and unsupervised learning, and (IV) emerging methods including foundation models (FM), diffusion-based generative AI, and quantum optimization. A literature synthesis of self-reported benchmarks shows that ML-based methods (Paradigm~III) report 95-99\% of model-based spectral efficiency at 10^2-10^4 x faster per-inference runtime (method-pair dependent; literature values are self-reported and exclude ML pre-training cost). A companion tutorial benchmark at N=16, N=64, and N=128 reveals a critical scaling property: GPU-based neural network inference (DDQN, PPO, graph neural network (GNN), unsupervised DL) is N-invariant, with identical runtime at N=16 and N=128, while iterative solvers (AO+SCA, PSO) scale polynomially. Energy efficiency (P2) and PAPR-constrained (P4) benchmarks are deferred to future work with standardized power models and waveform generators. Six open challenges emerge from the synthesis: the cross-paradigm benchmark deficit, real-world hardware-constrained deployment, joint waveform-RIS optimization for doubly-dispersive channels, multi-objective PAPR trade-offs, LLM safety in live network control, and diminishing returns of standalone heuristics. We specify requirements for a standardized benchmark. This study serves as a roadmap for researchers and practitioners working on joint OFDM-RIS optimization in 6G networks.
2606.31334.paper_summary_zh: 聯合 OFDM-RIS 優化在 6G 中是一個混合整數非線性規劃（MINLP）問題，涵蓋了總速率最大化、能源效率、最大最小公平性和峰均功率比（PAPR）約束目標。調查了 2021 年至 2026 年間發表的七十八篇聯合 OFDM-RIS 優化的研究。沒有標準化的基準存在，跨論文比較仍然不可行。本調查將這些工作分類為四種範式：（I）基於模型的凸放鬆，（II）啟發式和元啟發式搜索，（III）深度強化和無監督學習，以及（IV）新興方法，包括基礎模型（FM）、基於擴散的生成 AI 和量子優化。自我報告基準的文獻綜合顯示，基於機器學習的方法（範式 III）在每次推理運行時間上報告 95-99\% 的基於模型的頻譜效率，速度為 10^2-10^4 倍（方法對的依賴性；文獻值為自我報告，並不包括機器學習的預訓練成本）。在 N=16、N=64 和 N=128 的伴隨教程基準揭示了一個關鍵的縮放特性：基於 GPU 的神經網絡推理（DDQN、PPO、圖神經網絡（GNN）、無監督深度學習）是 N 不變的，在 N=16 和 N=128 時運行時間相同，而迭代求解器（AO+SCA、PSO）則以多項式方式縮放。能源效率（P2）和 PAPR 約束（P4）基準將推遲到未來的工作中，並使用標準化的功率模型和波形生成器。從綜合中出現了六個開放挑戰：跨範式基準赤字、現實世界硬體約束的部署、雙重色散通道的聯合波形-RIS 優化、多目標 PAPR 取捨、LLM 在實時網絡控制中的安全性，以及獨立啟發式的收益遞減。我們指定了標準化基準的要求。本研究為在 6G 網絡中從事聯合 OFDM-RIS 優化的研究人員和實踐者提供了一個路線圖。
2606.31334.author: Ahmet Kaplan et.al.
